title: [Bug]: Unable to use Native Overlayfs - Podman reverts back to fuse-overlayfs
body:
### Issue Description

Environment Info:

* `OpenShift (OKD) 4.12 on SCOS (CentOS Stream Core OS)`
* `Eclipse Che 7.58.0 enabled for rootless container builds`
* `CoreOS kernel version: 5.14.0-200.el9.x86_64`
*  `Podman 4.3.1`

I am testing using podman for rootless container builds within Eclipse Che (OpenShift Dev Spaces)

VFS is painfully slow, especially for container in container.

Fuse-overlayfs does not work in rootless container in container on OpenShift

I would like to leverage native overlayfs support but cannot seem to find any documentation on how to enable it.  

This really good post from 2021 is the nearest to a guide that I have found:  [https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-rootless-overlay](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-rootless-overlay)

Attempting a basic podman build fails because it tries to default to `fuse-overlayfs`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create an Eclipse Che workspace from: [https://github.com/cgruver/workspace-dev](https://github.com/cgruver/workspace-dev)
1. Open a terminal in the workspace after it starts
1. Execute: 
   `podman info`
1. Observe that there is no overlay helper configured:
   ```yaml
   store:
     configFile: /home/user/.config/containers/storage.conf
     containerStore:
       number: 0
       paused: 0
       running: 0
       stopped: 0
     graphDriverName: overlay
     graphOptions: {}
     graphRoot: /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage
     graphRootAllocated: 107363696640
     graphRootUsed: 37010493440
     graphStatus:
       Backing Filesystem: overlayfs
       Native Overlay Diff: "false"
       Supports d_type: "true"
       Using metacopy: "false"
   ```
1. execute `podman pull quay.io/cgruver0/che/podman-basic:latest`
1. Observe that it is successful
1. Create a Dockerfile:  
   `echo "FROM quay.io/cgruver0/che/podman-basic:latest" > Dockerfile`
1. Attempt to build a new image:
   `podman build -t test:test .`
1. Observe the failure:
   ```bash
   STEP 1/1: FROM quay.io/cgruver0/che/podman-basic:latest
   ERRO[0000] Unmounting /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/31ca8ae6b8c7cf62112c1f12ab961368f27041aa8b85bd406af412438384815f/merged: invalid argument 
   Error: mounting new container: mounting build container "6ee664ea51dce23daa109bbbf309b138fe3804db48ff7014e539d980452bccc4": creating overlay mount to /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/31ca8ae6b8c7cf62112c1f12ab961368f27041aa8b85bd406af412438384815f/merged, mount_data="lowerdir=/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/E5XLE6JRIJAZPFEK2WSFKO5SCO:/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/QEC2ELUKZJ7BYFTRYCON4WPQUT,upperdir=/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/31ca8ae6b8c7cf62112c1f12ab961368f27041aa8b85bd406af412438384815f/diff,workdir=/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/31ca8ae6b8c7cf62112c1f12ab961368f27041aa8b85bd406af412438384815f/work,,volatile": using mount program /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs: unknown argument ignored: lazytime
   unknown argument ignored: 
   fuse: device not found, try 'modprobe fuse' first
   fuse-overlayfs: cannot mount: No such file or directory
   : exit status 1
   ```
1. Observe that `.has-mount-program` file exists
   ```bash
   ls -al ~/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay
   drwx------.  5 user root  185 Jan 10 14:07 .
   drwx------. 10 user root 4096 Jan 10 13:44 ..
   -rw-------.  1 user root    4 Jan 10 14:07 .has-mount-program
   ```

If you execute `podman system reset` and try the above steps again, the same results are observed and the `.has-mount-program` file is recreated.

### Describe the results you received

1. Attempt to build a new image:
   `podman build -t test:test .`
1. Observe the failure:
   ```bash
   STEP 1/1: FROM quay.io/cgruver0/che/podman-basic:latest
   ERRO[0000] Unmounting /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/31ca8ae6b8c7cf62112c1f12ab961368f27041aa8b85bd406af412438384815f/merged: invalid argument 
   Error: mounting new container: mounting build container "6ee664ea51dce23daa109bbbf309b138fe3804db48ff7014e539d980452bccc4": creating overlay mount to /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/31ca8ae6b8c7cf62112c1f12ab961368f27041aa8b85bd406af412438384815f/merged, mount_data="lowerdir=/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/E5XLE6JRIJAZPFEK2WSFKO5SCO:/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/QEC2ELUKZJ7BYFTRYCON4WPQUT,upperdir=/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/31ca8ae6b8c7cf62112c1f12ab961368f27041aa8b85bd406af412438384815f/diff,workdir=/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/31ca8ae6b8c7cf62112c1f12ab961368f27041aa8b85bd406af412438384815f/work,,volatile": using mount program /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs: unknown argument ignored: lazytime
   unknown argument ignored: 
   fuse: device not found, try 'modprobe fuse' first
   fuse-overlayfs: cannot mount: No such file or directory
   : exit status 1
   ```


### Describe the results you expected

Successful image build

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

* `OpenShift (OKD) 4.12 on SCOS (CentOS Stream Core OS)`
* `Eclipse Che 7.58.0 enabled for rootless container builds`
* `CoreOS kernel version: 5.14.0-200.el9.x86_64`

### Additional information

Dockerfile for the `podman` container:

```bash
FROM quay.io/centos/centos:stream9

ARG USER_HOME_DIR="/home/user"
ARG WORK_DIR="/projects"

ENV HOME=${USER_HOME_DIR}
ENV _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS=""

# Note: compat-openssl11 & libbrotli are needed for che-code (Che build of VS Code)

RUN dnf install -y openssl compat-openssl11 libbrotli git tar which shadow-utils bash zsh wget jq podman buildah skopeo; \
    dnf update -y ; \
    dnf clean all ; \
    mkdir -p ${USER_HOME_DIR} ; \
    mkdir -p ${WORK_DIR} ; \
    chgrp -R 0 /home ; \
    #
    # Setup for root-less podman
    #
    setcap cap_setuid+ep /usr/bin/newuidmap ; \
    setcap cap_setgid+ep /usr/bin/newgidmap ; \
    touch /etc/subgid /etc/subuid ; \
    chmod -R g=u /etc/passwd /etc/group /etc/subuid /etc/subgid /home ${WORK_DIR} ; \
    echo user:10000:65536 > /etc/subuid  ; \
    echo user:10000:65536 > /etc/subgid

USER 10001
WORKDIR /projects
```

comments.body:
FWIW, It looks like podman is defaulting to fuse-overlayfs on a plain CentOS Stream system too.  Not container in container.

Kernel: 5.14.0-205.el9.x86_64

After running `podman system reset` the `.has-mount-program` file is recreated on the next podman command.

Here is the same build in an Eclipse Che workspace with debug:

Possibly interesting entries:

```bash

WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
```

that seems expected since overlay on top of overlay won't work.  You can use a volume to store the graph storage inside the first container and make sure its file system is not `overlay`.

OK.  So, for now is VFS really the only available option for OpenShift container-in-container?  It works.  It's just slow.  ;-)

Also...  if that graph storage idea is a possible solution, do you have a how-to or RTFM that you could point me to?  I understood the words, but have no idea what you are talking about.  :-)

No you have to mount a volume on the containers/storage directory to get it to work.

But you will have to run a more privileged container then the default security of OpenShift.

Thanks guys!  We'll stick with VFS for now.

I'll close this and go read Dan's new book.  ;-)
Hi, can we reopen it? I use podman inside a container and can't make it work with native overlayfs. The Kernel version is 5.12.2.
Others have made this work, so this is not a Podman issue.  What error are you seeing?  Did you setup a volume on top of containers/storage?

Hi, I run this [setup](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container) on my local machine (Kernel v6), and native overlay is not enabled. In production, we use podman inside a firecracker instance with a mounted volume for containers/storage (Kernel 5.12.2). But I can't make it work either locally or in production.
